title: Passive TFTP PUT Workflow
summary: The router acts as a TFTP server and the user or App uploads firmware with a TFTP client.
scope: cross-brand workflow; model parameters required before use

phases.title: Prepare client network
phases.purpose: Place the user's computer on the recovery subnet before attempting upload.
actions: Apply the profile's client IP assignment requirement.
Disable unrelated network paths if they interfere with routing.

phases.title: Enter recovery mode
phases.purpose: Put the router into the state where it may accept TFTP requests.
actions: Follow the model-specific recovery entry method.
Watch model-specific LED or network readiness signals.

phases.title: Upload firmware
phases.purpose: Send firmware to the router-side TFTP server.
actions: Use WRQ/PUT from the client to the router recovery IP.
Use the profile's firmware file type and filename rule.
Follow the server ACK source port during transfer.

readiness_signals.meaning: The device may be in or near a recovery window.
readiness_signals.warning: ICMP readiness does not guarantee that TFTP WRQ is ready.

readiness_signals.meaning: The router accepted the TFTP write request and transfer can proceed.
readiness_signals.warning: Client must continue to follow the server ACK source port.

failure_modes.symptom: TTL or ping is visible but TFTP PUT receives no ACK or times out.
likely_causes: ICMP readiness occurs before TFTP WRQ readiness
wrong network interface or routing path
failure_modes.recommended_response: Do not promote a model profile from ping alone. Capture an incident and retest with program-assisted WRQ timing.

panic_entry_queries: router recovery TFTP put no ACK

source_refs.notes: Shared vocabulary for Passive TFTP / TFTP PUT and Recovery Window.

notes: Draft workflow. Requires model-specific parameters and evidence before App use.
